title: SQL Injection Risk via Vulnerable Drizzle ORM Version
description: The backend package manifest package.json specifies drizzle-orm version ^0.38.0. Drizzle ORM versions below 0.45.2 contain a vulnerability where SQL identifiers are not properly escaped, exposing the database to potential SQL injection under specific usage conditions.
root_cause: drizzle-orm dependency in package.json does not properly escape SQL identifiers in versions below 0.45.2, allowing potential SQL injection attacks if dynamic identifiers are used in queries.
intended_behavior: The database framework should securely escape all identifiers and values in generated SQL queries.

trace.description: package.json lists drizzle-orm version ^0.38.0 as a project dependency
The application loads the vulnerable package version during server boot

conditions.description: drizzle-orm is used to query database models

instructions: Audit package.json to identify vulnerable packages
Find dynamic query builders utilizing unescaped identifiers
Execute injection payloads on target fields
execution.expected_result: Improper escaping of queries leading to SQL syntax execution errors or data leakage

remediation.strategy: Upgrade drizzle-orm package to version 0.45.2 or higher.

likelihood.reason: Queries in the codebase are currently parameterized and do not use dynamic identifiers directly from inputs.

impact.reason: A successful SQL injection leads to complete data exposure or database compromise.

confidence.reason: Confirmed by public CVE advisory and local npm audit check.

title: Cleartext Default Database Credentials in Version Control
description: Default PostgreSQL password sz_secure_vault_pass_99 and Master Vault Secrets are defined in plain text in .env and src/db/seed.ts files. Pushing these default keys to version control poses a risk of secrets exposure if environments are not properly overridden.
root_cause: seed in src/db/seed.ts does not load production secret keys dynamically from external environment variables, allowing default cleartext credentials to persist.
intended_behavior: All database and API credentials must be injected dynamically via environment parameters and never hardcoded in configurations or source code.

trace.description: A hardcoded bearer token string is defined in seed.ts
The hardcoded credential is encrypted and written to the postgres database

conditions.description: No secure environment override provided during database seeding operations

instructions: Inspect version control history for .env and seed.ts files
Retrieve the default credentials and connection strings
Connect externally to the exposed database using the retrieved keys
execution.expected_result: Successful authentication and administrative access to the database container

remediation.strategy: Override all passwords, secrets, and bearer tokens in production using secure env vars and never commit default keys in raw form.

likelihood.reason: Default keys are likely to be used if the administrator does not explicitly configure environment overrides.

impact.reason: Exposes database contents and administrative roles to any party with repo visibility.

confidence.reason: Confirmed by direct inspection of seed.ts and .env files.

title: Loose Regular Expression Parsing in PII Privacy Gateway
description: The PII gateway privacy-gateway.ts uses simplified regex patterns for email, card, and SSN formats. Sophisticated or non-standard inputs can bypass the filters, leaking unmasked PII data to external LLM providers.
root_cause: maskPayload in src/utils/privacy-gateway.ts does not use comprehensive regex boundaries, allowing modified PII formats to bypass masking.
intended_behavior: The privacy gateway should sanitize all PII formats, ignoring variation formatting details like spaces or special casing.

trace.description: Simple regular expressions are defined for sensitive data types
The simplified regex replaces text, potentially leaving non-standard formats unmasked

conditions.description: PII data is passed in modified or non-standard formats (e.g., spaces in card numbers or special characters in emails)

execution.attacker_perspective: User entering non-standard PII formatted input
instructions: Input sensitive card information containing spaces
Submit payload to the invoke endpoint
Inspect the request sent to the external LLM provider
execution.expected_result: PII is sent to external LLMs without being intercepted or masked by the gateway

remediation.strategy: Deploy exhaustive regular expression sets or dedicate a parser to filter all variations of PII formats.

likelihood.reason: Modified PII formats like spaced credit cards are commonly entered by users.

impact.reason: Exposes sensitive PII/SPI to external LLM providers, breaching privacy compliance bounds.

confidence.reason: Confirmed by analyzing regex structures in privacy-gateway.ts.

title: Exposed Database and Backend Container Ports
description: The docker-compose.yml configuration maps port 5622:5432 and 3055:3055 directly to the host network interface. On a VPS without firewall restrictions, this exposes the postgres database and backend API to the public internet.
root_cause: ports configuration in docker-compose.yml does not bind mapped ports to 127.0.0.1, allowing open host interfaces to bind to port 5622 and 3055.
intended_behavior: All internal container ports should only bind to local loopback interfaces or container networks unless public exposure is explicitly intended.

remediation.strategy: Bind mapped ports strictly to localhost (127.0.0.1) in docker-compose.yml configurations.

conditions.description: The host system VPS does not run firewall rules to block port 5622 and 3055

instructions: Perform TCP port scan on port 5622 and 3055
Send PostgreSQL connection handshake request to port 5622
execution.expected_result: Database container responds to connection requests from public IP addresses

likelihood.reason: VPS environments frequently lack customized firewall profiles on secondary ports.

impact.reason: Exposes database port directly to global brute force and injection attempts.

confidence.reason: Confirmed by auditing docker-compose.yml ports mappings.
